code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test12-MAY-2017Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, potassium, and chloride, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), supporting possible renal dysfunction, such as acute or chronic kidney disease, with possible tubular involvement. No evidence of urinary tract infection is present. Further nephrological evaluation is recommended.

coding.display: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus

Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
